text: Is a 'capitalism of rules' (Milei/Argentina) genuinely distinct from populism, or is the style/substance distinction a way of laundering disruptive market politics that share populist features?
context: Tension between Daiana's defense of Milei as non-populist and the moderator's framing of Milei alongside Trump and Musk's chainsaw politics; never resolved.

addressed_in_artifacts.voice_name: Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
